description: Educational model of the Canadian criminal court process from arrest to resolution. Approximately 12 distinct appearances/stages in a typical cannabis-related criminal matter.
disclaimer: Educational model only. Your specific case may vary. Not legal advice.

appearances.description: Initial court appearance. Bail set or denied. Right to counsel (s.10b) critical here.
Crown ordered to provide full disclosure. Jordan clock may start here.
Defence reviews crown disclosure for gaps, Charter issues, investigative deficiencies.
Negotiation with Crown. Withdrawal, diversion, or reduced resolution discussed.
Judge-facilitated discussion of issues, potential guilty plea, or trial efficiency.
Election of trial by judge alone, judge+jury, or preliminary inquiry request.
s.8 (search), s.9 (detention), s.10b (counsel), s.11b (delay) motions served.
Evidence heard on constitutional violations. Exclusion of evidence (s.24(2)) argued.
Crown calls witnesses. Cross-examination. Charter remedies may be applied.
Defence calls evidence if applicable. Expert witnesses, medical necessity arguments.
If convicted: sentencing submissions. If acquitted or sentenced: appeal rights.

trial: s.11b — right to trial in reasonable time

jordan_framework.note: Delay above ceiling presumptively unreasonable. Crown must justify or charges stayed.
